rule_text: For regional leisure trips, Nancy tends to choose ground transportation instead of flying.
canonical_answer: For trips like my autumn getaways, I usually prefer to go by road or rail rather than book a flight.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would check flight options first for intercity travel.

evidence.content: taking autumn road trips to Québec City’s historic districts

rule_text: When choosing stops for a new trip, Nancy favors places centered on nature and local food culture over generic tourist attractions.
canonical_answer: I like trip stops that immerse me in landscapes, markets, and local food rather than standard tourist checklists.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would pick the most famous or highest-rated stops regardless of theme.

evidence.content: balances a love for natural immersion and artistic discovery
exploring the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore’s sea caves and culinary markets in Duluth

rule_text: If part of a trip falls through, Nancy prefers replacement stops that preserve the original nature-or-culture theme of the trip.
canonical_answer: When a stop changes, I want the substitute to keep the same feel as the original trip rather than just fill the gap.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would swap in any convenient or highly rated alternative stop.

evidence.content: Because she favors thoughtful travel that balances natural immersion and artistic discovery, a disruption should be repaired with same-theme replacements rather than unrelated substitutes.

rule_text: When making restaurant reservations, Nancy prefers quiet, intimate places over loud or high-energy venues.
canonical_answer: I’d rather book somewhere quiet and intimate than a bustling spot.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would choose restaurants based mainly on popularity, trendiness, or broad availability.

evidence.content: preferring small gatherings where deep conversation can flourish
attending intimate acoustic sets by Bon Iver at the Dakota Café

rule_text: For dining out, Nancy tends to favor restaurants with seasonal, locally sourced menus over generic options.
canonical_answer: I usually choose places that feel seasonal and locally rooted, especially if they highlight good ingredients.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would pick any convenient restaurant that fits the time and party size.

evidence.content: values sustainability, creativity, and quiet contribution to the collective well-being
participation in local farmers’ markets
develop a pilot line of functional foods featuring locally grown Minnesota ingredients

rule_text: When selecting events to attend, Nancy prefers intimate local arts or acoustic events over mass-market spectacles.
canonical_answer: I’m much more likely to choose a small local art or acoustic event than a huge flashy production.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would choose whatever event is biggest, most famous, or most heavily promoted.

evidence.content: attending intimate acoustic sets by Bon Iver at the Dakota Café
In the evenings, she attends intimate art openings, pottery workshops, and live acoustic sets at local cafés

rule_text: When booking a personal class or wellness appointment, Nancy prefers low-key local studios or craft-oriented providers over generic commercial services.
canonical_answer: I usually book with local, community-feeling providers instead of big generic service chains.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would choose the most convenient or cheapest provider regardless of atmosphere.

evidence.content: morning tai chi at a local studio affiliated with the Twin Cities Yoga Collective
pottery skills in weekend workshops led by local artisan Anna Smith

rule_text: If a service booking is for a fad-like wellness offering, the agent should check with Nancy before committing.
canonical_answer: If it’s some trendy wellness thing rather than an established class or service, ask me first before booking it.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would go ahead and book the appointment if it generally matched her interests.

evidence.content: Her background in microbiological testing, statistical analysis, and food-safety regulation suggests she is evidence-minded and unlikely to want surprise bookings for trend-driven wellness services.

rule_text: Because she sometimes forgets appointments, Nancy prefers reminders to be set for scheduled activities rather than relying on memory alone.
canonical_answer: I like a reminder attached to appointments because I do sometimes forget them.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would simply create or keep the appointment without adding any reminder.

evidence.content: even as they occasionally forget appointments

rule_text: For recurring classes or volunteer activities, Nancy prefers weekly reminders rather than one-off alerts.
canonical_answer: For things I do regularly, a weekly reminder works better for me than a single reminder.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would typically set a one-time reminder or no recurring reminder at all.

evidence.content: they maintain a low-key fitness routine of morning tai chi
she volunteers monthly at a community garden

rule_text: When writing messages that represent her work or projects, Nancy prefers to draft them first rather than send them immediately.
canonical_answer: I like to draft important messages first so I can make sure they’re clear and well-phrased.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would send the message directly once the content was available.

evidence.content: Her communication strengths enable her to distill technical findings into clear reports and concise presentations

rule_text: When an invitation conflicts with unresolved plans, Nancy prefers replying tentatively instead of accepting or declining outright.
canonical_answer: If I’m not sure yet, I usually respond as tentative instead of forcing an early yes or no.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would either accept immediately or decline to clear the calendar.

evidence.content: they are relaxed about deadlines and occasionally disorganized
even as they occasionally forget appointments

rule_text: For purchases connected to food, gardening, or household ingredients, Nancy prefers locally sourced or regenerative products when available.
canonical_answer: I usually choose products with local or regenerative sourcing over generic alternatives.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would choose based mostly on price, convenience, or broad ratings.

evidence.content: values sustainability
develop a pilot line of functional foods featuring locally grown Minnesota ingredients
guides regional farms and artisanal producers toward regenerative practices

rule_text: If something she bought does not work out, Nancy prefers an exchange over a refund so the item can still be put to use.
canonical_answer: If a purchase misses the mark, I usually prefer exchanging it rather than just asking for a refund.
counterfactual_default: A person without this rule would usually request a refund to get the money back.

evidence.content: values sustainability, creativity, and quiet contribution to the collective well-being
Her sustainability-oriented, practical farm-rooted background makes replacement and continued use more appealing than treating the item as disposable.
